question: Is the general environment (busy intersection, traffic lights, crowd) rendered in a photorealistic style?
Is the woman walking the dog rendered in an Impressionist style with visible blurry brushstrokes?
Is the Golden Retriever rendered in a photorealistic style, distinct from the Impressionist woman?
Does the leash show a visible style transition, changing from hazy/Impressionist near the hand to realistic near the dog collar?
Is the cyclist rendered in a Cubist style featuring fragmented geometric shapes?
Is the bike-share bicycle ridden by the Cubist cyclist rendered in a photorealistic style?
Are the puddles and water droplets splashing from the wheels rendered in a photorealistic style?
Is the delivery worker on the crosswalk rendered in a Pop Art style with bold outlines?
Is the delivery box carried by the Pop Art delivery worker rendered in a photorealistic style?
Is the aroma mist wafting from the delivery box rendered in a semi-transparent watercolor style?
Is the newsstand at the corner rendered in a photorealistic style?
Is the fox crouching on the newsstand rendered in a Ukiyo-e (Japanese woodblock print) style?
Does the Ukiyo-e fox's tail hang down and partially obscure the realistic newsstand sign?
Are all four distinct art styles (Impressionist, Cubist, Pop Art, Ukiyo-e) clearly distinguishable within the single image?
